sections.paragraph:
-

(1) to contract, to draw together, almost the same as אָסַף and קָפָא; Arab. قلص (the letters ט and צ being interchanged). Part. pass. קָלוּט a dwarf, any thing of contracted stature or size, Lev 22:23. (Arab. قَلَطِىُّ, قُلَاطُ a dwarf, see Kamûs, p. 965, قَلْطُ low stature, قَلِيطُ (Saad. loc. cit.) one suffering from hernia.)

(2) to receive a fugitive to oneself, i.q. Ch. קְלַט. Derivatives, מִקְלָט, and pr.n. קְלִיטָה.
